records.ety: [OE. aventure, aunter, anter, F. aventure, fr. LL. adventura, fr. L. advenire, adventum, to arrive, which in the Romance languages took the sense of “to happen, befall.” See Advene.]

defs: That which happens without design; chance; hazard; hap; hence, chance of danger or loss.
quotes: Nay, a far less good to man it will be found, if she must, at all adventures, be fastened upon him individually.
He was in great adventure of his life.

defs: The encountering of risks; hazardous and striking enterprise; a bold undertaking, in which hazards are to be encountered, and the issue is staked upon unforeseen events; a daring feat.
A remarkable occurrence; a striking event; a stirring incident; as, the adventures of one's life.
A mercantile or speculative enterprise of hazard; a venture; a shipment by a merchant on his own account.

records.ety: [OE. aventuren, auntren, F. aventurer, fr. aventure. See Adventure, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Adventured (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Adventuring (<?/).]

defs: To risk, or hazard; jeopard; to venture.
quotes: He would not adventure himself into the theater.

defs: To venture upon; to run the risk of; to dare.
quotes: Yet they adventured to go back.

defs: To try the chance; to take the risk.
quotes: I would adventure for such merchandise.
